title: Best CS2 Workshop Maps — Aim Training, Movement & Utility Practice | CSGOLuck Wiki
description: Curated directory of the best CS2 Steam Workshop maps for aim training, movement practice, utility lineups, warmup routines, and more. Links to subscribe directly.
heading: Best CS2 Workshop Maps
intro: A curated collection of {MAPS.length} essential Steam Workshop maps for improving at Counter-Strike 2. From aim training to surf, utility lineups to movement practice.

faqs.question: How do I install CS2 workshop maps?
faqs.answer: Open Steam, go to the CS2 Workshop page, find the map you want, and click "Subscribe". The map will automatically download. Then in CS2, go to Play → Practice → Workshop Maps to find and load it.

faqs.question: What is the best aim training map for CS2?
faqs.answer: Aim Botz by ulletical is the most popular aim training map, used by professional players worldwide. It provides stationary and moving bots in a controlled environment. For reflex training, Fast Aim/Reflex Training is also excellent.

faqs.question: Can I practice smokes on workshop maps?
faqs.answer: Yes. Yprac makes dedicated utility practice maps for each active duty map (Mirage, Inferno, Dust2, etc.) that show you exactly where to stand and aim for every common smoke, flash, and molotov lineup.

faqs.question: What workshop maps do pros use for warmup?
faqs.answer: Most pros warm up with Aim Botz for 10-15 minutes, then play a few rounds of community FFA Deathmatch. Some also use the Yprac Prefire maps to practice angle clearing on specific maps they will play.

faqs.question: Are surf and KZ maps available in CS2?
faqs.answer: Yes, surf and KZ (climb) maps have been ported to CS2. While the movement physics differ slightly from CSGO, the core mechanics work. Look for maps labeled CS2 compatible in the workshop.
